troparion.text: Your thirty-three holy martyrs, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text: The radiant choir of martyrs, the light-bearing company, / spiritually descends to us, enlightening the Church with brilliant rays. / Therefore as we celebrate their honored memory, / we ask You, our Savior: / through their prayers deliver us from misfortunes / so that we may sing to You: “Alleluia.”

saints.name: Venerable Lazarus, Wonderworker of Mount Galesius Near Ephesus

troparion.text: You watered your pillar with prayerful vigils and flowing tears; / you brought forth fruit a hundredfold with sighs from the depths of your soul. / You were a shepherd, granting the requests of all. / Entreat Christ God, venerable Lazarus our father, that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text: With joy the Church of Christ glorifies you with psalms / as a most brilliant light, venerable Lazarus. / Therefore, always pray to Christ that He may grant forgiveness of transgressions to all.

saints.name: The Discovery of the Relics of Venerable Zosimas, Abbot of Vorbozomsk

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Zosimas, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

saints.name: Finding of the relics of Venerable Cyril, Abbot of Novoezersk, Vologda

troparion.text: You appeared as a bright lamp at New Lake on Beautiful Island, Venerable Father Cyril,  / for you took the Cross of Christ upon your shoulders,  / and followed Him diligently, with purity toward God, /  drawing closer to Him and were adorned with the power to work miracles; / therefore, we come to the shrine of your revered relics, humbly crying out: /  "O Venerable Father Cyril, pray to Christ God for the salvation of our souls."

saints.name: Martyrs Melasippus, Karina, their son, Antoninus, and forty children converted by their martyrdom, at Ancyra

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

saints.name: Martyrs Auctus, Taurion, and Thessalonica, at Amphipolis, in Macedonia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

saints.name: “Joyful” Icon of the Mother of God

troparion.text: Let all of us, who are burdened with sins, fall on our knees before the Theotokos,  /  bowing before her wonderworking Icon of Tenderness,  /  kissing it and crying aloud with tears: / “O Sovereign Lady, accept the prayer of your unworthy servants,  /  and grant to us who ask, your great mercy.”

kontakion.text: We have no other help, we have no other hope but you, O Lady.  /  Help us, for in you do we hope,  /  and of you do we boast, for we are your servants.  /  Let us not be put to shame.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Theodotus, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Theodotus;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.
